items.title: Building townhouses on a residential Canberra block could soon be easier

children.content: Townhouses, terraces and low-rise apartments will be allowed on all ACT residential blocks under a major change to planning rules proposed by the government.

items.title: 'Is that a UFO?' Suspected meteor lights up night sky

children.content: Colourful streaks across the sky have been spotted across eastern Australia as a "fireball" meteor passed through the atmosphere.

image.altText: Dashcam footage showing a flashing appearing across the sky

items.title: What you need to know about ACT's public school strike

children.content: For the first time in 15 years, ACT public school staff have gone on strike in a two-hour work stoppage that saw all public schools, including specialist schools and preschools, keep their doors closed until 10:30am on Friday.

image.altText: School bags in the ACT

items.title: 'Classes of 50 children': Full-day ACT public teacher strike flagged

children.content: Canberra public schools were closed until 10:30am on Friday, with more industrial action expected in June.

items.title: Fears CSIRO job cuts will damage Australia's climate change adaptability

children.content: Peak body Science and Technology Australia is concerned the CSIRO's plan to cut 92 jobs, predominantly from its environment and climate science division, will negatively affect Australia's ability to assess and adapt to climate change.

image.altText: The CSIRO logo on a sign

items.title: Million-dollar cigarette plot tied to Canberra grandmother's killer

children.content: New details have emerged about the violent 1999 home invasion that claimed Irma Palasics's life, including a separate million-dollar cigarette conspiracy that put one of her killers behind bars a decade later.

image.altText: An old photo of a man and a woman, nicely dressed, sitting next to each other smiling.

items.title: Is Canberra's CBD unsafe? It depends who you ask

children.content: Concerns about anti-social behaviour, construction and poor lighting are shaping perceptions of safety in Civic, but locals remain divided about whether Canberra's CBD feels unsafe after dark.

gallery.altText: An unknown person walks past a graffiti tunnel.

thumbnail.altText: An unknown person walks past a graffiti tunnel.

items.title: The mission to save conjoined twin involved an airlift, five surgeons and a heartbreaking sacrifice

children.content: A team of doctors involved in the separation of conjoined Papua New Guinean twins Sawong and Tom have spoken for the first time about the rare and complex case.

gallery.altText: A close up of a Papua New Guinean baby smiling from a cot.

thumbnail.altText: A close up of a Papua New Guinean baby smiling from a cot.
Conjoined twins lie together on a small cot.

items.title: Children's entertainer Danny Go!'s son dies of cancer aged 14

children.content: The popular children's YouTuber revealed the news after documenting the illness since it was diagnosed late last year. Tributes have flowed from Children's entertainers, including the Wiggles.

image.altText: The YouTuber shared the news of his son's diagnosis in December, last year.

items.title: Pendlebury honoured before record-breaking game, Roos beat Suns after the siren

children.content: North Melbourne comes from 43 points back to beat Gold Coast after the siren before attention turns to the MCG, where Collingwood's Scott Pendlebury will break the V/AFL games record against West Coast. Follow live.

image.altText: Scott Pendlebury smiles on the field before a game

items.title: How the Musk v Altman case revealed an 'uncomfortable' truth

children.content: After Sam Altman and OpenAI's recent victory in a drawn-out legal battle with billionaire Elon Musk, the bitter rivalry between the former friends is now set to move from the courtroom to Wall Street.

gallery.altText: Elon Musk sits on chair on stage next to Sam Altman and an interviewer.

thumbnail.altText: Elon Musk sits on chair on stage next to Sam Altman and an interviewer.

items.title: The budget's central weakness was revealed this week

children.content: As debate over the federal government's budget tax hit on wealth and assets continues to reverberate, one can't help but wonder how it's going down among the gen Zs and millennials it's designed to impress.

image.altText: Men in suits speak at a press conference

items.title: Brent Harvey is about to lose his place in AFL history

children.content: Brent 'Boomer' Harvey has enjoyed being the AFL/VFL games record holder for the past decade, and says he could have played more if he'd accepted an offer from other clubs to extend his career.

image.altText: Brent Harvey smiles while sitting on an armchair. He wears a black t-shirt.

items.title: Black mould and broken toilets: Inside 'dismal' government-owned homes

children.content: Bug infestations, walls covered in black mould and broken toilets are just some of the issues plaguing homes in Australia's most isolated communities, where the landlord is the state government.

gallery.altText: Black mould and insects cover a wall and ceiling around Christian statues.
A profile of an Aboriginal woman looking to her left with grass in background and road with potholes

thumbnail.altText: Black mould and insects cover a wall and ceiling around Christian statues.
A profile of an Aboriginal woman looking to her left with grass in background and road with potholes

items.title: For Willie Rioli now is time to 'give back' to the place that gave him everything

children.content: The Tiwi Islands are unique and Australian Rules football is a way of life that has spawned some of the game's biggest names. But even as favourite son Willie Rioli Jr returns home, the Tiwi Bombers know they need more help.

image.altText: A man sits on the grass shirtless next to other footy players. Some other men are standing in the background.

items.title: 'Beautiful, kind, caring man' dies in hospital months after alleged bashing

children.content: Friends of Darwin man Martin Wiberg, 48, are remembering him as a "very gentle soul" after he died in hospital this week.

image.altText: Man wearing sunglasses through a hole in a cement wall

items.title: Suzie Miller took the risky path away from a safe job as a judge, with no regrets

children.content: In 2009, Suzie Miller had a choice between becoming a magistrate in Australia or a residency at the National Theatre in London.

gallery.altText: a woman with tousled blonde hair in a black top
Two women and a man in formal wear pose with trophies on a red carpet

thumbnail.altText: a woman with tousled blonde hair in a black top
Two women and a man in formal wear pose with trophies on a red carpet

avatar.alt: Brett Worthington stands in a courtyard inside parliament house

items.title: The perfect warm winter salad using crisp-roasted artichokes and grains

children.content: Crispy artichokes and textural grain make this salad a satisfying dinner and excellent next-day lunch.

image.altText: A grey serving bowl of salad, with browned artichoke hearts on top, sits on a bench.

items.title: The prickly plant powering a new spirits industry

children.content: Australia's emerging agave industry is attracting interest from distillers, farmers and scientists who believe the spiky plant could transform everything from tequila production to sustainable aviation fuel.

image.altText: Close up of an agave plant.

items.title: Elon Musk's SpaceX launches upgraded Starship

children.content: Elon Musk's rocket company has launched its new Starship design to enable more frequent Starlink satellite launches and to send future NASA missions to the moon.

image.altText: Take off of SpaceX's Starship V3

gallery.altText: People looking over the ocean watching a rocket take off

thumbnail.altText: People looking over the ocean watching a rocket take off

items.title: Police investigate triple fatal crash in Sydney's south

children.content: Three people have died in a car crash in Sydney's south, with police attempting to conduct a welfare check at one of the victim's homes minutes before the incident

image.altText: Police and fire cars parked near a crashed car.

items.title: Tulsi Gabbard resigns as Trump's director of national ​intelligence

children.content: Tulsi Gabbard says she is departing from the role to support her husband after his recent bone cancer diagnosis.

image.altText: A woman wearing white speaks into a microphone as she looks diagonally to her right

items.title: ABC 50/50 quiz: Test your knowledge of Prince, Twin Peaks and some very important donkeys

children.content: Each question in the ABC 50/50 quiz has only two options: one is right; the other is wrong. But there's a catch.

items.title: Queensland jockey dies in hospital after training accident

children.content: Both of Shane McGovern's legs were amputated during a weeks-long stay in hospital.

image.altText: A male jockey atop a horse in a country area.

items.title: When the bushfire came, the Walkers turned on their roof sprinkler and left

children.content: Preparing for an ember attack could extend your home's bushfire survivability rate to above 90 per cent or even closer to 100 per cent, a fire scientist says.

gallery.altText: Aerial perspective of a coastal home spinkler system on roof surrounded by burned trees under a blue sky.
A couple sit beside one another at a wooden table in their living room with green walls.

items.title: Psychiatrist repeatedly harassed nurse on messaging platforms

children.content: The only psychiatrist in a regional WA area is given a more lenient sanction for sexually harassing a nurse, partially due to a lack of other mental health specialists in the area.

image.altText: A close-up shot of a doctor noting down symptoms of a patient on a form with a stethoscope hanging around his neck.

items.title: Is Australia's two-party political system dead?

children.content: With the rise of One Nation's popularity and younger voters increasingly turning to the Greens, the traditional two-party system in Australian politics has been dismantled, a former political strategist argues.

image.altText: Composite of Greens volunteers holding placards and Pauline Hanson meeting One Nation voters

items.title: Giant iceberg with shipping containers spotted off Antarctica

children.content: Details of how the shipping containers ended up on a giant iceberg have been revealed in one of the more unusual reports tabled at the annual meeting of Antarctic Treaty nations.

Shipping containers sitting on a massive iceberg.
A research station on pylons in Antarctica.

items.title: Collingwood champion's achievement reopens old wound outside Victoria

children.content: When Scott Pendlebury steps onto the MCG on Saturday afternoon, his name will stand alone at the top of a remarkable list — but the achievement has also reignited a debate about the value of non-Victorian Aussie Rules records.

image.altText: Craig Bradley moves the ball forward for South Australia.

items.title: Call to 911 reveals harrowing moments before NASCAR champ's death

children.content: A call to 911 reveals NASCAR champion Kyle Busch had been coughing up blood in the hours before his tragic death aged 41.

image.altText: GettyImages-2277717923

items.title: Tony Albert's form of optimism is a lesson for us all

children.content: Girramay, Yidinji and Kuku-Yalanji artist Tony Albert hopes to "take every item of 'Aboriginalia' in Australia out of circulation".

gallery.altText: Tony Albert smiles with arms crossed across chest. He wears bright purple shirt and blurred window is behind him.

thumbnail.altText: Tony Albert smiles with arms crossed across chest. He wears bright purple shirt and blurred window is behind him.

items.title: WA health official wants more support to stem 'rare' diphtheria outbreak

children.content: The director of the Communicable Disease Control Directorate says vaccines help prevent more serious symptoms, but more efforts are needed to tackle the diphtheria outbreak in remote areas.

image.altText: A nurse preparing a boostrix vaccination, the vaccine's white and purple box is visible in the foreground.

items.title: WHO upgrades risk assessment for Ebola outbreak in DRC to 'very high'

children.content: The World Health Organization raises its assessment of the outbreak going nation-wide as a province in the Congo's east bans funerals and suspends the local football league.

image.altText: A group of workers in head-to-toe medical protective gear walk in a line as the lead worker sprays a substance on the ground.

items.title: Does Queensland's premier have his attack dog on a leash?

children.content: Queensland's deputy premier made a 'dick' joke about his political opponent this week, but the difference in his approach and the premier's raises questions, writes political reporter Jack McKay.

image.altText: Jarrod Bleijie and David Crisafulli

avatar.alt: ABC Queensland state political reporter Jack McKay

items.title: Queensland man charged by anti-terrorism police

children.content: A 48-year-old has been arrested by the state's Joint Counter Terrorism Team over alleged "concerning posts" on social media.

image.altText: Uniformed AFP officers standing in a line
